claim: RL (Reinforcement Learning) when applied to LLMs doesn't generalize well beyond the training distribution, making it 'peaky' and limited to specific tasks it was trained on
actionable: When using RL for LLMs, design product+model co-design so that economically useful tasks are brought into the training distribution rather than expecting the model to generalize to arbitrary tasks
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: The 2017-2022 RL research era had widespread overfitting to benchmarks at a meta-community level, not just individual papers, causing many methods to not pan out despite appearing successful in academic literature
actionable: When evaluating RL research or methods, scrutinize whether gains come from genuine algorithmic improvements or from tuning knobs implicitly fit to specific benchmarks; prioritize reproducibility on out-of-distribution tasks
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: Robotics people are the most grounded at NeurIPS because they work with real-world constraints; simulation people are the most detached from reality
actionable: When hiring or evaluating AI researchers, prioritize those with hands-on experience with real-world systems over pure simulation specialists, as they develop better intuitions for what actually matters
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: The reasoning team at OpenAI grew from about 12 people when o1 started to 300+ people by o3, indicating massive organizational scaling required for advanced reasoning models
actionable: Budget for significant organizational scaling (25x growth) if building production reasoning models; this requires investment in safety, evals, and multi-disciplinary expertise
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: IOI (International Olympiad in Informatics) Gold and IMO (International Mathematical Olympiad) achievements by language models haven't translated to broad AI impact despite massive capabilities improvements
actionable: Don't use benchmark achievements alone to predict real-world impact; validate against actual economically useful tasks and user workflows before scaling
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: Cursor's ML team (20-25 people) built high-quality reasoning models through product-model co-design, enabling policy updates every 2 hours instead of the multi-month cycles typical at larger labs
actionable: Adopt tight product-model co-design loops with rapid iteration cycles (2-hour policy updates) by keeping ML and product teams small, focused, and geographically co-located
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: Context-switching away from AI agents to do manual work kills productivity (described as giving you 'ADHD'); speed and staying in the loop with the model is critical
actionable: Optimize for agent speed over raw capability; if an agent takes >5 minutes to respond, users will context-switch to manual work and lose flow, even if the agent eventually gets better answers
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: Continual learning with infinite memory (models learning from deployment tokens and storing them in weights without overloading capacity) may be a paradigm shift bigger than current reasoning models
actionable: Explore continual learning architectures that can efficiently integrate deployment-time feedback into model weights; treating weights as a hard drive with measurable capacity could unlock major efficiency gains
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: Academic RL research often rewards complex math ideas with many tuning knobs over simple ideas that work; this incentive structure contributed to RL winter
actionable: In research evaluation and hiring, weight empirical results and reproducibility over mathematical novelty; favor simple methods with fewer hyperparameters and clearer generalization
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor

claim: Large labs (like OpenAI) find it hard to ship rapid product-model co-design (2-hour updates) because product and ML teams operate independently and developers get 'context-switch ADHD'
actionable: If building coding agents, adopt a startup structure with tightly integrated product-ML teams; avoid traditional org silos where product and ML operate independently
source_title: [State of RL/Reasoning] IMO/IOI Gold, OpenAI o3/GPT-5, and Cursor Composer — Ashvin Nair, Cursor
